title: FTS5-fallback LIKE escaper omits backslash, diverging from canonical helper

findingDetails.evidence: queryCausalEdgesLikeFallback escapes raw user query with `query.replace(/[%_]/g, '\\$&')` (only % and _) then binds `%${escaped}%` into `content_text LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\' OR title LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\'` (lines 273,278). The canonical escapeLikePattern() in vector-index-types.ts:29 escapes backslash FIRST (\\->\\\\) before % and _; this site does not.
findingDetails.why: With ESCAPE '\\' active, a literal backslash in the query becomes a dangling escape introducer. If followed by a non-wildcard char, SQLite raises a runtime LIKE error (the .all() at line 282 is not wrapped in try/catch in the read excerpt, so it propagates); if at a wildcard boundary it silently alters matching. The epic's stated remediation was that all LIKE patterns route through one shared escaped helper — this hand-rolled escaper was missed.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace `query.replace(/[%_]/g, '\\$&')` with the shared escapeLikePattern(query) from vector-index-types.ts so backslash is neutralized consistently with every other LIKE site; keep the existing ESCAPE '\\' clause.
